_meta.notes: Captured via in-repo TaskCompleted shim (pact-plugin/tests/runbooks/install_taskcompleted_logging_shim.sh) during in-session capture experiment on 2026-05-20. This is the TEAMMATE-frame TaskCompleted stdin shape: 10 keys, with `teammate_name` and `team_name` identifying who owned the completing task. The empirical presence of `teammate_name` IS the teammate-context signature under the wake-lifecycle layer's compound field-presence check (`agent_id is None and teammate_name is None` returns False when teammate_name is present). Sibling-shape relationship: this shape EQUALS the lead-frame shape (taskcompleted_lead_context_shape.json, 8 keys) plus exactly two fields — `teammate_name` and `team_name` — per the `delta_from_lead_shape` _meta key. A future reader comparing the two fixtures should see no other field-level differences. Sanitized: real session_id/transcript_path/cwd/task_subject/task_description/team_name replaced with example-placeholder values; payload shape preserved verbatim.

task_subject: example teammate-driven TaskCompleted fire — captures the teammate-frame stdin shape for fixture-parity testing
task_description: Example task description for a teammate-driven TaskUpdate(status="completed") fire. The fire originates from a teammate completing its own task; the empirical stdin shape carries `teammate_name` identifying the agent that owned the task plus `team_name` identifying the lead team. Used as the canonical teammate-frame fixture for TaskCompleted hook tests.
